name: create-item
description: Creates a new item in a Fabric workspace. Use this when the user wants to create a Lakehouse, Notebook, or other Fabric item type. Requires workspace ID, item name, and item type.

option.name: --tenant
option.description: The Microsoft Entra ID tenant ID or name. This can be either the GUID identifier or the display name of your Entra ID tenant.

option.name: --auth-method
option.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'credential' (Azure CLI/managed identity), 'key' (access key), or 'connectionString'.

option.name: --retry-delay
option.description: Initial delay in seconds between retry attempts. For exponential backoff, this value is used as the base.

option.name: --retry-max-delay
option.description: Maximum delay in seconds between retries, regardless of the retry strategy.

option.name: --retry-max-retries
option.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations before giving up.

option.name: --retry-mode
option.description: Retry strategy to use. 'fixed' uses consistent delays, 'exponential' increases delay between attempts.

option.name: --retry-network-timeout
option.description: Network operation timeout in seconds. Operations taking longer than this will be cancelled.

option.name: --workspace-id
option.description: The ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --workspace
option.description: The name or ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --display-name
option.description: The display name for the item.

option.name: --item-type
option.description: The type of the Fabric item (e.g., Lakehouse, Notebook, etc.).

option.name: --description
option.description: The description for the item.

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.

name: list_files
description:
List files and directories in OneLake storage using a filesystem-style hierarchical view, similar to Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2. 
Shows directory structure with paths, sizes, timestamps, and metadata. Use this to explore OneLake content in a filesystem format 
rather than flat blob listing. Supports optional path filtering and recursive directory traversal.

If no path is specified, intelligently discovers content by searching both Files and Tables folders automatically,
providing comprehensive visibility across all top-level OneLake folders.

Use --format=raw to get the unprocessed OneLake DFS API response for debugging and analysis.

option.name: --tenant
option.description: The Microsoft Entra ID tenant ID or name. This can be either the GUID identifier or the display name of your Entra ID tenant.

option.name: --auth-method
option.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'credential' (Azure CLI/managed identity), 'key' (access key), or 'connectionString'.

option.name: --retry-delay
option.description: Initial delay in seconds between retry attempts. For exponential backoff, this value is used as the base.

option.name: --retry-max-delay
option.description: Maximum delay in seconds between retries, regardless of the retry strategy.

option.name: --retry-max-retries
option.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations before giving up.

option.name: --retry-mode
option.description: Retry strategy to use. 'fixed' uses consistent delays, 'exponential' increases delay between attempts.

option.name: --retry-network-timeout
option.description: Network operation timeout in seconds. Operations taking longer than this will be cancelled.

option.name: --workspace-id
option.description: The ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --workspace
option.description: The name or ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --item-id
option.description: The ID of the Fabric item.

option.name: --item
option.description: The name or ID of the Fabric item. When using friendly names, MUST include the item type suffix (e.g., 'ItemName.Lakehouse', 'ItemName.Warehouse').

option.name: --path
option.description: The path to list in OneLake storage (optional, defaults to root).

option.name: --recursive
option.description: Whether to perform the operation recursively.

option.name: --format
option.description: Output format for OneLake API responses. Use 'json' for parsed objects, 'xml' for raw XML API response, or 'raw' for unprocessed API response. Supported values: 'json' (default), 'xml', 'raw'.

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.

name: list_items_dfs
description: List OneLake items in a workspace using the OneLake DFS (Data Lake File System) data API.

option.name: --tenant
option.description: The Microsoft Entra ID tenant ID or name. This can be either the GUID identifier or the display name of your Entra ID tenant.

option.name: --auth-method
option.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'credential' (Azure CLI/managed identity), 'key' (access key), or 'connectionString'.

option.name: --retry-delay
option.description: Initial delay in seconds between retry attempts. For exponential backoff, this value is used as the base.

option.name: --retry-max-delay
option.description: Maximum delay in seconds between retries, regardless of the retry strategy.

option.name: --retry-max-retries
option.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations before giving up.

option.name: --retry-mode
option.description: Retry strategy to use. 'fixed' uses consistent delays, 'exponential' increases delay between attempts.

option.name: --retry-network-timeout
option.description: Network operation timeout in seconds. Operations taking longer than this will be cancelled.

option.name: --workspace-id
option.description: The ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --workspace
option.description: The name or ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --recursive
option.description: Whether to perform the operation recursively.

option.name: --continuation-token
option.description: Token for retrieving the next page of results.

name: list_workspaces
description: Lists all Fabric workspaces accessible via OneLake data plane API. Use this when the user needs to view available workspaces or select a workspace for data operations. Returns workspace names and IDs.

option.name: --tenant
option.description: The Microsoft Entra ID tenant ID or name. This can be either the GUID identifier or the display name of your Entra ID tenant.

option.name: --auth-method
option.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'credential' (Azure CLI/managed identity), 'key' (access key), or 'connectionString'.

option.name: --retry-delay
option.description: Initial delay in seconds between retry attempts. For exponential backoff, this value is used as the base.

option.name: --retry-max-delay
option.description: Maximum delay in seconds between retries, regardless of the retry strategy.

option.name: --retry-max-retries
option.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations before giving up.

option.name: --retry-mode
option.description: Retry strategy to use. 'fixed' uses consistent delays, 'exponential' increases delay between attempts.

option.name: --retry-network-timeout
option.description: Network operation timeout in seconds. Operations taking longer than this will be cancelled.

option.name: --continuation-token
option.description: Token for retrieving the next page of results.

option.name: --format
option.description: Output format for OneLake API responses. Use 'json' for parsed objects, 'xml' for raw XML API response, or 'raw' for unprocessed API response. Supported values: 'json' (default), 'xml', 'raw'.

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.

name: upload_file
description: Uploads a file to OneLake storage from inline content or local file path. Use this when the user needs to store data in OneLake. Supports overwrite control and content type specification.

option.name: --tenant
option.description: The Microsoft Entra ID tenant ID or name. This can be either the GUID identifier or the display name of your Entra ID tenant.

option.name: --auth-method
option.description: Authentication method to use. Options: 'credential' (Azure CLI/managed identity), 'key' (access key), or 'connectionString'.

option.name: --retry-delay
option.description: Initial delay in seconds between retry attempts. For exponential backoff, this value is used as the base.

option.name: --retry-max-delay
option.description: Maximum delay in seconds between retries, regardless of the retry strategy.

option.name: --retry-max-retries
option.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations before giving up.

option.name: --retry-mode
option.description: Retry strategy to use. 'fixed' uses consistent delays, 'exponential' increases delay between attempts.

option.name: --retry-network-timeout
option.description: Network operation timeout in seconds. Operations taking longer than this will be cancelled.

option.name: --workspace-id
option.description: The ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --workspace
option.description: The name or ID of the Microsoft Fabric workspace.

option.name: --item-id
option.description: The ID of the Fabric item.

option.name: --item
option.description: The name or ID of the Fabric item. When using friendly names, MUST include the item type suffix (e.g., 'ItemName.Lakehouse', 'ItemName.Warehouse').

option.name: --file-path
option.description: The path to the file in OneLake.

option.name: --content
option.description: The content to write to the file.

option.name: --local-file-path
option.description: The path to a local file to upload.

option.name: --overwrite
option.description: Whether to overwrite existing files.

option.name: --content-type
option.description: MIME content type to set on the uploaded file (e.g., 'application/json'). Defaults to 'application/octet-stream'.

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.

name: info
description: Displays running MCP server information.

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.

name: plugin-telemetry
description:
Publish plugin-related telemetry events from agent hooks.
Accepts command-line options such as '--timestamp', '--event-type', '--session-id', '--client-type', '--client-name', 
'--plugin-name', '--plugin-version', '--skill-name', '--skill-version', '--tool-name', and '--file-reference'. 
Use this command from agent hooks in clients like VS Code, Claude Desktop, or Copilot CLI to emit usage metrics.

option.name: --timestamp
option.description: Timestamp of the telemetry event in ISO 8601 format.

option.name: --event-type
option.description: Type of event being logged (e.g., 'skill_invocation', 'tool_invocation', 'reference_file_read').

option.name: --session-id
option.description: Session identifier for correlating related events.

option.name: --client-type
option.description: Type of client invoking the telemetry (e.g., 'copilot-cli', 'claude-code', 'vscode'). Deprecated: prefer --client-name.

option.name: --client-name
option.description: Name of the client invoking the telemetry (e.g., 'copilot-cli', 'claude-code', 'Visual Studio Code', 'Visual Studio Code - Insiders').

option.name: --plugin-name
option.description: Name of the plugin being invoked.

option.name: --plugin-version
option.description: Version of the plugin being invoked.

option.name: --skill-name
option.description: Name of the skill being invoked.

option.name: --skill-version
option.description: Version of the skill being invoked.

option.name: --tool-name
option.description: Name of the tool being invoked.

option.name: --file-reference
option.description: Plugin-relative file reference being accessed (will be validated against an allowlist).

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.

name: start
description: Starts Azure MCP Server.

option.name: --transport
option.description: Transport mechanism to use for MCP Server.

option.name: --namespace
option.description: The service namespaces to expose on the MCP server (e.g., storage, keyvault, cosmos).

option.name: --mode
option.description: Mode for the MCP server. 'single' exposes one azure tool that routes to all services. 'namespace' (default) exposes one tool per service namespace. 'all' exposes all tools individually.

option.name: --tool
option.description: Expose only specific tools by name (e.g., 'acr_registry_list'). Repeat this option to include multiple tools, e.g., --tool "acr_registry_list" --tool "group_list". It automatically switches to "all" mode when "--tool" is used. It can't be used together with "--namespace".

option.name: --read-only
option.description: Whether the MCP server should be read-only. If true, no write operations will be allowed.

option.name: --debug
option.description: Enable debug mode with verbose logging to stderr.

option.name: --dangerously-disable-http-incoming-auth
option.description: Dangerously disables HTTP incoming authentication, exposing the server to unauthenticated access over HTTP. Use with extreme caution, this disables all transport security and may expose sensitive data to interception.

option.name: --dangerously-disable-elicitation
option.description: Disable elicitation (user confirmation) before allowing high risk commands to run, such as returning Secrets (passwords) from KeyVault.

option.name: --outgoing-auth-strategy
option.description: Outgoing authentication strategy for service requests. Valid values: NotSet, UseHostingEnvironmentIdentity, UseOnBehalfOf.

option.name: --dangerously-write-support-logs-to-dir
option.description: Dangerously enables detailed debug-level logging for support and troubleshooting purposes. Specify a folder path where log files will be automatically created with timestamp-based filenames (e.g., azmcp_20251202_143052.log). This may include sensitive information in logs. Use with extreme caution and only when requested by support.

option.name: --dangerously-disable-retry-limits
option.description: Dangerously disables upper bounds on retry delays, max delays, network timeouts, and max retries. This may lead to excessively long waits and should only be used when explicitly needed.

option.name: --cloud
option.description: Azure cloud environment for authentication. Valid values: AzureCloud (default), AzureChinaCloud, AzureUSGovernment, or a custom authority host URL starting with https://

option.name: --disable-caching
option.description: Disable caching of resource responses, requiring repeated requests to fetch fresh data each time.

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.

name: list
description:
List all available commands and their tools in a hierarchical structure. This command returns detailed information
about each command, including its name, description, full command path, available subcommands, and all supported
arguments. Use --name-only to return only tool names, and --namespace to filter by specific namespaces.

option.name: --namespace-mode
option.description: If specified, returns a list of top-level service namespaces instead of individual tools.

option.name: --namespace
option.description: Filter tools by namespace (e.g., 'storage', 'keyvault'). Can be specified multiple times to include multiple namespaces.

option.name: --name-only
option.description: If specified, returns only tool names without descriptions or metadata.

option.name: --include-hidden
option.description: If specified, includes hidden commands in the output.

option.name: --learn
option.description: Discover available sub-commands and their parameters without executing any Azure operation. Use on a command group (e.g. 'azmcp storage --learn') to list all commands in that group, or on a specific command (e.g. 'azmcp storage account list --learn') to see its options.
